questions.question: A 45-year-old man presents with hoarseness. On laryngoscopy, the vocal cord on the right side is fixed in a paramedian position. Which nerve is most likely damaged?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. External laryngeal nerve

questions.question: A patient with a thyroidectomy develops weakness of phonation. Which muscle is supplied by the external laryngeal nerve?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Cricothyroid

questions.question: A 30-year-old woman presents with difficulty breathing and loss of voice after neck surgery. Laryngoscopy reveals both vocal cords loss of abduction. Which muscles is likely to injured?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Posterior cricoarytenoid

questions.question: A 55-year-old woman presents with difficulty breathing and loss of voice after neck surgery. Laryngoscopy reveals both vocal cords with loss of adduction. Which muscles is likely to injured?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Lateral cricoarytenoid

questions.question: A patient presents with a foreign body sensation in the throat. On examination, a fish bone is lodged in the sides of the laryngeal inlet. Where is the bone lodged?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Pyriform fossa

questions.question: A 60-year-old man is diagnosed with laryngeal cancer involving the true vocal cord. Which structure forms the true vocal cord?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Cricothyroid

questions.question: A patient with a laryngeal tumor is found to have involvement of the rima glottidis. What is the rima glottidis?
options: Space between the epiglottis and the thyroid cartilage
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Space between the vocal cords

questions.question: A 40-year-old man presents with a thyroid nodule. Which cartilage is most prominent in the neck and forms the Adam's apple?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Thyroid cartilage

questions.question: A patient with a laryngeal injury is found to have damage to the internal laryngeal nerve. Which part of the larynx is affected?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Above the vocal folds

questions.question: A 35-year-old man presents with a laryngeal tumor. The laryngeal inlet is bounded anteriorly by which structure?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Epiglottis

questions.question: A young singer at the local music theater visits her physician and complains of vocal difficulties. On examination, she is unable to abduct the vocal cords during quiet breathing. Which of the following muscles is most likely paralyzed?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Posterior cricoarytenoid muscle

questions.question: A 3-year-old healthy boy underwent a laryngoscopy that revealed a large fish bone emerging from the left piriform fossa. The bone was removed during anesthesia induction. While removing the fishbone, the piriform fossa was perforated. Which of the following structures was injured?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. The internal laryngeal nerve

questions.question: A 26-year-old singer visits her physician—an ear, nose, and throat (ENT) surgeon—and complains of changes in her voice. A laryngoscope examination demonstrates a lesion of the superior laryngeal nerve, causing weakness of which of the following muscles?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Cricothyroid muscle

questions.question: A young singer at the local music theater visits her physician and complains of vocal difficulties. On examination, she is unable to abduct the vocal cords during quiet breathing. Which of the following muscles is most likely paralyzed?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Posterior cricoarytenoid muscle

questions.question: A 62-year-old woman singer at the local music theater visits her physician and complains of vocal difficulties. On examination, she is unable to adduct the vocal cords during quiet breathing. Which of the following muscles is most likely paralyzed?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Lateral cricoarytenoid muscle

questions.question: A 28-year-old man has had thyroid surgery to remove his papillary carcinoma. The external laryngeal nerve is damaged during the surgery. This injury could result in a severe impairment of function of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Tensing the vocal cords

questions.question: A 38-year-old man underwent thyroid surgery to remove his papillary carcinoma. The external laryngeal nerve, which runs alongside the superior thyroid artery, was damaged during the surgery. This injury could lead to a severe impairment of which of the following functions?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Tensing the vocal cords.

questions.question: A 71-year-old woman often visits an emergency department with swallowing difficulties and subsequent choking while eating food. Which of the following pairs of muscles is most instrumental in preventing food from entering the larynx and trachea during swallowing?
options: Inferior pharyngeal constrictor and thyrohyoid muscles
Levator veli palatini and tensor veli palatini muscles
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Oblique arytenoid and aryepiglottic muscles

questions.question: A 32-year-old woman has hoarseness in her voice on phonation. Which of the following nerves is damaged?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. External laryngeal nerve

questions.question: A 12-year-old boy has difficulty breathing because he is choking on food. A school nurse performs the Valsalva maneuver to expel air from his lungs and thus dislodge the food. When that fails, she performs a needle cricothyrotomy, which would open into which of the following regions?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Infraglottic cavity
